ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #7, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Feb-28-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Avid Alavi appeared by zoom for Thomas Lucas (Trustee).

Mrs. Alavi presented oral argument to support her client's request for a 1% fee. The court expressed that Mrs. Alavi's advocacy is stale since Borel Private Bank doesn't exist anymore, noted that the prior payment of fees was at a rate of $200.00 per hour or more, and stated that it uses the rate of $145.00 per hour as a guideline. The court also questions whether this advocacy is worth $450.00 an hour. Mrs. Alavi raised the issue of the fiduciary taking on a lot of liability. The court stated that 1% is not reasonable in a special needs trust. The Seventh Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: F. Ruiz. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Jan-21-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Avid Alavi appeared by zoom for and with Thomas Lucas by zoom.

Ms. Alavi stated that the time entry titled "accounting preparation worker" was actually for preparation of the accounting schedules, that it's the number of hours it takes to prepare the accounting schedules and that it is her understanding that her Client uses an experienced probate bookkeeper John Arnold just as so many other Professional Fiduciaries do. Ms. Alavi also addressed the Trustee's fees stating that she filed a supplement on January 4th, that her Client bears the sole responsibility and liability of this case, that often times these matters result in actions where the Fiduciary has to defend out of his own funds, spoke of the costs of doing business in San Francisco, and stated that the hourly rate of $145.00 does not allow Professional Fiduciaries to cover the overhead costs and make a living. In addition, Ms. Alavi stated that other counties such as San Mateo and Contra Costa have much higher hourly rates and are based on the experience of the Fiduciary, that Mr. Lucas has over twenty years of experience as a Professional Fiduciary, that he has thirty five years of social work experience, and that his level of education, knowledge, creativity and experience allows him to manage this case efficiently. The Court suggested that Counsel contact the Probate Director and stated that it has a responsibility to make sure fees are reasonable. The Sixth Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-08-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrew Schultz for Thomas Lucas (Trustee).

Mr. Schultz stated that the property was originally valued at $210,000.00 as of August 26, 2009, that it was not adjusted every year, that there has been a significant increase in the value and that the property was re-appraised as of the end of the accounting date of August 31, 2016. In addition, Mr. Schultz stated that rents have increased, that his Clients overlook has increased and that his Client has expenses that need to be captured. Furthermore, Mr. Schultz stated that he has not looked at his Clients time sheets, that fiduciaries try to strike a balance and that they are sometimes unpaid, and that he was told by Mr. Wong (Referee) that they don't discount for fractional interests. The Fourth Account and Report of Trustee, Etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: L. Maloney. Not reported.
